systems.name: lazysite
systems.tagline: Markdown files served and cached on the fly by a small CGI script - no build, no database.
systems.best_for: Content-led and documentation sites, AI-assisted publishing, and anyone who wants a fast, low-maintenance site they fully own - with dynamic features and paid pages when they are needed.

systems.name: Hugo
systems.tagline: A very fast static-site generator that compiles Markdown to static files with Go templates.
systems.best_for: Large, purely static sites maintained by developers who want a build pipeline, version-controlled deploys and a big theme ecosystem.

rows.label: Build step
rows.lazysite: None - renders on request, caches the result
rows.hugo: Yes - rebuild the whole site on every change

rows.label: Dynamic content
rows.lazysite: Server-side templating, remote data, query parameters
rows.hugo: Static only - needs external JavaScript services

rows.label: Built-in forms, auth & payments
rows.lazysite: All built in (forms, sign-in, x402 pay-per-read)

rows.label: AI-first management
rows.lazysite: Built for it - agents edit over WebDAV, an API or MCP
rows.hugo: Edit the source, then build and deploy

rows.label: Themes & customisation
rows.lazysite: Layout plus theme tokens - one file to re-skin

rows.label: Hosting
rows.lazysite: Any CGI host, or its own built-in server

rows.label: Performance
rows.lazysite: Cached static HTML after the first hit

rows.label: Backup
rows.lazysite: Copy the files - nothing else to dump
rows.hugo: Copy the source files - nothing else

rows.label: Migration & portability
rows.lazysite: Move the folder anywhere - plain files
rows.hugo: Move the source folder - plain files

rows.label: Maintenance & security surface
rows.lazysite: Small - static files, no database, a compact codebase

rows.label: Editing experience
rows.lazysite: Markdown files, a simple manager, or an AI agent
rows.hugo: Markdown files plus a build step
